summary: Feature #3 — Video Comments: full backend + frontend coverage. 16/16 pytest tests green. Frontend admin & viewer UI flows verified against the public preview URL (post / list / delete with window.confirm; viewer can only delete own; per-video scoping confirmed via API + UI).

issues: Radix warning: Missing Description or aria-describedby for DialogContent. Add <DialogDescription> or aria-describedby for a11y. (Existing issue, not introduced by Feature #3.)

critical_code_review_comments: routes/comments.py list_all_comments: limit query param is unbounded on the lower side (limit=0 or negative would call to_list(0) returning []). Add a Query(ge=1) or default-floor it. Minor.

routes/comments.py add_comment: Pydantic min_length=1 already prevents '' but allows '   '. The runtime strip+400 fallback is correct, but the 422 from pydantic for body completely missing/empty has a less friendly message than the 400 'Comment cannot be empty' from whitespace-only. Consider validating with a Pydantic validator that strips first to make the error consistent (single 400 with the same detail).
VideoComments.jsx: load() swallows ALL errors (catch {} comment says only 404 expected). A 401/network error will leave the user with a permanently blank list; surface a small inline error state.

VideoComments.jsx: comment list re-fetch is not triggered after delete in case server-side returned an error mid-flight; only optimistic state.filter is used. If multiple tabs are open this can drift. Acceptable for now but worth noting.
routes/comments.py: no rate limiting on POST. A malicious authenticated user can flood comments. Consider per-user throttling at gateway level or a simple in-memory limiter.

VideoLibrary.jsx line 343: <VideoComments> mount is gated by selectedVideo && showPlayer, but selectedVideo can stay set while showPlayer toggles; ensures unmount on close — good. No issue, just call out the dependency.

Admin moderation list is mounted under GET /api/comments — there is no UI surface for it yet (component-level moderation page). Backend is ready; frontend feature for moderation UI is out of scope for this iteration but worth tracking.
test_credentials: Admin: StreamHost / TestPass123!@# ; Viewer: testviewer / Viewer123!
seed_data_creation: Tests post and clean up TEST_-prefixed comments on the existing 'PlayLab Test' video (id 2ff9939e-53da-41eb-9edc-e9a9e602b4ca). All TEST_ comments were swept via admin DELETE in teardown. To enable the in-app player for UI testing, the same video's processing_status was temporarily flipped from 'external' to 'ready' in MongoDB (test_database.videos) and RESTORED back to 'external' after the run — DB is back to original state.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Feature #3 (Video Comments) fully verified end-to-end. Backend tests live in /app/backend/tests/test_comments.py. Frontend Play button is gated on processing_status === 'ready'; only one such video exists in the seed (PlayLab Test was external — temporarily ready'd during test, then restored). For future UI tests on the player dialog, either (a) seed a real ready video, or (b) flip a video's processing_status in DB and restore after. Admin moderation UI for /api/comments is not yet wired in the frontend — backend is ready when E1 chooses to add a moderation page. Window.confirm is used for delete; Playwright must register page.on('dialog', accept) before clicking delete.
